prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial begins on a nearly black/dark green screen with archive watermarks visible, including a red “Classic Commercial Archives” logo at lower left and a faint “@ClassicCommercialArchives” watermark across the lower middle.

It then cuts to an outdoor garden setting. An older woman with light gray styled hair is shown seated, smiling and looking toward the left side of the frame. She wears a light blue cardigan over a pale top. Dense greenery fills the background, with small colorful flowers or garden lights visible. She appears to have a light-colored fluffy object or small dog in her lap, though the sampled frame does not make this fully clear.

The next shot shows an older woman, likely the same person, now standing on a lawn in the same or similar garden. She wears a mustard/yellow jacket over dark clothing. Her arms are extended as if moving or playing. A small brown dog stands behind her on the grass. The setting includes trees, shrubs, potted flowers, and a landscaped border.

In the final frame, the woman is seen from behind or side-back, with one arm extended as if throwing, swinging, or playing with the dog. The small brown dog runs across the lawn. Large pink text appears over the garden scene reading: “Get back in the swing.” On the right side of the frame, a large blue bottle with a white-and-pink label is displayed prominently; the most readable label text is “M-O.” The archive watermarks remain visible.
